title: supabase_to_hugo_sync_architecture
decision: One-way idempotent sync: cephas_content_registry → Hugo content files. Category-to-dir mapping hardcoded in sync script. Front-matter generated from registry metadata. content_markdown written as body. Sync also regenerates founder/anecdotes.md from anecdotes table. Log: Cephas/sync_log.jsonl.
why: Supabase is canonical; Hugo is Relic. The sync bridge maintains Hugo from the canonical source without inverting the relationship. Idempotent: safe to run repeatedly.

title: cephas_content_registry_238_rows_13_categories
decision: cephas_content_registry holds 238 rows across 13 categories: article(62), outreach_letter(52), crown_letter(26), academic_paper(31), pudding(28), pitch(17), system_design(11), open_letter(4), founder(2), business-plan(2), under_the_hood(2), tribute(1), +others. This is the full canonical Cephas content inventory as of K522.6.
why: Provides a canonical baseline for the K522.6 sync implementation. Any future content class additions need a new entry in CATEGORY_DIR map in sync script.

title: hugo_213_orphan_files_legacy_relic
decision: Hugo has 213 files with no Supabase match. These are legacy content from before the Supabase-canonical decision. They are NOT deleted by the sync. They are flagged in sync_log.jsonl. The majority are in under-the-hood/ (internal architecture docs) and architecture/ (legacy design docs).
why: The orphaned files have archival value and may be publicly linked (though unlikely for under-the-hood/). The sync only writes forward, never deletes backward. Phase 2 deprecation will redirect all Hugo URLs to React SPA equivalents regardless.

title: anecdotes_seeded_13_rows_complete
decision: Supabase anecdotes table now has 13 rows: K404 seeds (The Shop, Triple Double) + K522.5 (To Blave) + K522.6 (10 numbered Hugo anecdotes: Paper Route, Intramural Giants, Roommate Suit, Pizza for Ice Cream, USAA Lifeline, Bridge Builder, Kurt Ikard, Golden Eagle's Head, Pet Antibiotics, Squad Car Mannequin).
why: The 10 numbered anecdotes were in Hugo Relic but missing from Supabase. K522.6 closes the gap. Founder expected 28+; remaining ~15 likely in Pudding archive (K522.7 candidate).

title: hugo_build_1468_pages_zero_errors
decision: Hugo v0.152.2 builds 1468 pages in 6.9s from the synced content with zero errors. This confirms the sync script generates valid Hugo front-matter and content for all 238 registry rows.
why: Hugo build success is the empirical gate for Phase B.3. 1468 pages = 238 synced rows + ~1200 existing Hugo pages + aliases + paginator pages.

title: graceful_deprecation_three_phase_plan
decision: Phase 1 (post-launch): Hugo active, sync runs weekly. Phase 2 (4-8 wk): Hugo serves 301 redirects only, sync stopped, React SPA serves content. Phase 3 (12+ wk): Hugo retired, DNS redirects to React SPA, cephas-hugo/ archived. Trigger for each phase is explicit Founder ratification, not a date timer.
why: The three-phase plan gives public links time to update in search engines before Hugo goes dark. 301 redirects in Phase 2 preserve SEO equity for any indexed Hugo URLs.

title: sync_slug_subpath_handling
decision: cephas_content_registry slugs can contain forward slashes (e.g. pudding/zero-storage-full-income). The sync script must call mkdirSync(pathDirname(filePath), {recursive: true}) before writeFileSync, not just mkdirSync on the category dir. Bug surfaced at first run, fixed in K522.6.
why: The slug is used as the relative file path within the category dir. If the slug contains a subpath, the intermediate directory must be created. This is a general pattern for any slug-based file generation system.

title: cephas_sync_on_demand_cadence_rationale
decision: Sync cadence is on-demand (not scheduled) until launch. Reason: (a) content changes are infrequent and deliberate, (b) no cron/GitHub Action overhead, (c) Founder/Bishop know when Supabase content changes and can trigger manually. Re-evaluate for weekly schedule if content change rate increases during launch sequence.
why: Scheduled sync would add operational complexity (auth token rotation, monitoring) for marginal benefit when content changes are deliberate events. On-demand is simpler and less failure-prone.
